title: Fallen Land - a detailed unboxing and reboxing of the second edition
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=_Jlf2Q0xGZ8

John Longgren of Fallen Dominion sent Daniel (@dungeondive) a comp copy of **Fallen Land Second Edition** with the Descendants expansion and the neoprene mat, so the channel runs a full unboxing and a giveaway in the same video — the original first-edition copy plus both first-edition expansions and Descendants goes to a Patron, the second edition stays with Daniel.

The bigger box turns out to be the right size: not bloated, just generous enough to swallow Descendants when you're done. Highlights of what's new beyond the cards-and-art touch-ups: the **scenario book** (competitive, co-op, solo, and campaign-stitching variants — Trail Blazers, Sigma Bunker Hunt, Pandemic, Pirate Radio, the 13th Faction campaign, and a fistful of optional rules like Ronin, Harsh Reality, and the timed game), a perfected version of the already-good rulebook, and the same first-player turn-order sheet that runs the whole pace of play.

Components are upgraded: 3D resource tokens, new clip-on faction trackers for the boards, restyled and more cohesive character art (a frequent first-edition complaint), a vibrantly recoloured map with glowing-orange irradiated zones, and a board that — crucially — is *not* meaningfully bigger than first edition. Daniel's reboxing demo confirms everything from base game, both first-edition expansions, and Descendants packs comfortably into a single Kallax-friendly footprint.

----

Daniel singles out Fallen Dominion's commitment to *retail availability after Kickstarter* as one of the reasons he keeps championing the game — no FOMO, anyone can buy in. Are there other small publishers you've watched do this well, and does it change how you back?
